title: Cutting resilient networks -- complete binary trees
abstract: In our previous work, we introduced the random $k$-cut number for rooted graphs. In this paper, we show that the distribution of the $k$-cut number in complete binary trees of size $n$, after rescaling, is asymptotically a periodic function of $\lg n - \lg \lg n$. Thus there are different limit distributions for different subsequences, where these limits are similar to weakly 1-stable distributions. This generalizes the result for the case $k = 1$, i.e., the traditional cutting model, by Janson.
